title: Debugging, Review, and Adversarial Prompting · Prompt Forensics
summary: Diagnose a failing build and review the repair against a base branch. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in an evidence-first debug and review playbook and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Capture a minimal reproduction, exact error, environment, and last-known-good boundary before repair.
Rank competing hypotheses and choose discriminating checks.
Prompt for the smallest cause-level repair and a regression test.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.instruction:
debug case — Use a changed debug case for prompt debug review that tests this competency: Capture a minimal reproduction, exact error, environment, and last-known-good boundary before repair.. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair
steps.instruction: Append a contract headed "Evidence: prompt-adversarial-cases-1gfwhr" with concrete Goal, Context, Constraints, Output, and Done when sections tailored to this case.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Adversarial Cases into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
Evidence: prompt-adversarial-cases-1gfwhr
Goal: [replace with one observable outcome]
Context: [replace with decision-relevant facts]
Constraints: [replace with authority and safety boundaries]
Output: [replace with the artifact and evidence shape]
Done when: [replace with changed-case acceptance criteria]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.
